academic_requirements.summary: Applicants need a secondary-school qualification valid for university entry. International applicants must complete a preliminary evaluation on apply.unive.it; the programme is open access, but non-EU applicants requiring a study visa must already hold an accepted mathematics/reasoning test result before submitting the application.
academic_requirements.prior_degree_or_diploma: Secondary school leaving qualification eligible for university admission; for international qualifications, at least 12 years of schooling are required.
academic_requirements.subject_requirements: The programme requires adequate knowledge of mathematics, logic, and reading comprehension. For EU/applicants not requiring a visa, TOLC-I is recommended and a low or missing score results in a logic-mathematics OFA. For non-EU applicants requiring a visa, an SAT or CISIA TOLC-I/CEnT-S result is required before application.
academic_requirements.gpa_or_country_requirements: Country-specific validity rules apply under Ca' Foscari's 'Entry requirements for applicants with an international qualification'; applicants with less than 12 years of schooling must document how the gap is covered.
academic_requirements.committee_or_pre_evaluation: The Welcome Unit carries out a preliminary evaluation of international qualifications on apply.unive.it; Ca' Foscari states that applicants proven eligible then have their entry requirements and academic background evaluated as required by the programme.

accepted_proofs: A recognised B2-or-higher English certification from Ca' Foscari's official list, such as Cambridge FCE, IELTS 5.5-6.5, TOEFL iBT 72-94, or PTE Academic 59-75.
A certificate from Ca' Foscari CLA or from a university language school included in Ca' Foscari's recognised-certifications list.
Proof that the applicant falls within an official exemption category.
exemption_rules: Completed higher-education studies or at least one year of college in a country where English is the medium of instruction.
Obtained a high-school or university qualification from a course taught entirely in English.
Completed at least one full year of high-school exchange in English.
Holds one of the listed degree exemptions in languages or linguistic mediation that includes a three-year English course.
language_requirements.assessment_or_upload_stage: Proof is uploaded during enrolment; Ca' Foscari does not test English during the application procedure.
language_requirements.deadline: By enrolment; otherwise an English OFA is assigned and must be fulfilled by 30 September 2027.

rounds: Applications for 2026/2027 open from 17 December 2025 on apply.unive.it, but the exact admission rounds visible to EU/equivalent applicants depend on the citizenship category selected on the portal [uncertain].
application_deadline_eu.enrolment_deadline: Enrol from 1 July 2026 to 30 September 2026; additional late window from 1 October 2026 to 30 October 2026 with a 50 EUR surcharge.
application_deadline_eu.notes: The programme is open access. Ca' Foscari states that deadlines for applicants with an international qualification differ from those for applicants with an Italian qualification and are category-based on apply.unive.it. [uncertain]

rounds: Applications for 2026/2027 open from 17 December 2025 on apply.unive.it, but the exact round deadline for non-EU applicants requiring a study visa is shown only after selecting the correct applicant category on the portal [uncertain].
application_deadline_non_eu.visa_or_pre_enrolment_deadline: Use the deadline shown on apply.unive.it for the non-EU study-visa category; the public programme page does not state a fixed programme-specific visa deadline [uncertain].
application_deadline_non_eu.current_status: The 2026/2027 admission-rounds article is published, but the exact Computer Science deadline for non-EU visa applicants is category-based on apply.unive.it [uncertain].
application_deadline_non_eu.notes: Non-EU applicants requiring a visa must already have an SAT or CISIA TOLC-I/CEnT-S result before applying. Ca' Foscari notes that the third CEnT-S period/result is too late for non-EU applicants requiring a visa and the fourth period/result is too late for any applicant with an international qualification.

required_documents.document_name: Valid passport or ID card, or receipt of renewal/first request
required_documents.notes: European applicants may use an ID card; international applications without the mandatory identification document are rejected.

required_documents.document_name: Valid residence permit or receipt of renewal/first request
required_documents.notes: Required for non-European applicants already residing in Italy or in the Schengen Area.

required_documents.document_name: Secondary-school mark sheet or transcript of records for at least the last year of study
required_documents.notes: If the applicant is still attending the last year of secondary school, a provisional mark sheet/transcript is mandatory; official English or Italian translations are required when needed.

Optional at application stage if not yet available, but the final document must be uploaded before the end of the enrolment period if a provisional document was used; translations are required when needed.

required_documents.document_name: Professional English or Italian translations of diplomas/transcripts
required_documents.notes: Required when the original documents are not in English or Italian; translations may be issued by professional translators, courts, or court interpreters.

required_documents.document_name: Statement of Comparability, Declaration of Value, ARDI Statement of Correspondence plus CIMEA Statement of Verification, or Diploma Supplement
required_documents.notes: These are not mandatory during the application procedure but are highly recommended because they support the evaluation of the qualification.

required_documents.document_name: Recognised English B2 certificate or proof of exemption
required_documents.notes: Required for the Data Science curriculum, which is taught in English.
Required before submitting the application for non-EU applicants requiring a study visa.

entry_exam_or_test.test_name: CISIA TOLC-I for EU/applicants not requiring a visa; SAT or CISIA TOLC-I/CEnT-S accepted for non-EU applicants requiring a study visa
entry_exam_or_test.applies_to: TOLC-I is recommended for EU/applicants not requiring a visa and affects OFA assignment; a pre-application SAT or CISIA TOLC-I/CEnT-S result is required for non-EU applicants requiring a study visa.
entry_exam_or_test.dates: CEnT-S periods for 2026/2027: 27 November 2025, 16 December 2025, 15 January 2026; 19 February 2026, 26 February 2026, 12 March 2026; 23 April 2026, 21 May 2026, 9 June 2026; 17 September 2026, 15 October 2026. Ca' Foscari says the third-period result is too late for non-EU visa applicants and the fourth-period result is too late for any applicant with an international qualification.
entry_exam_or_test.scoring_or_threshold: For EU/applicants not requiring a visa, if you do not sit the TOLC-I or score less than 20/40, you may still enrol but you receive a logic-mathematics OFA; science and English sections are excluded from this total.

source_quotes.quote: Only the Data Science curriculum is taught in English.
must have attended school for at least 12 years
requires a minimum B2-level knowledge of the English language
must have already taken an online written entry test

uncertainty_notes: Ca' Foscari publishes the 2026/2027 admission-round framework on apply.unive.it, but the exact round deadlines shown to international applicants depend on the citizenship category selected on the portal.
For non-EU applicants requiring a study visa, the public programme page states the accepted tests but does not publish a single fixed programme-specific application deadline.
